# Corpus Coverage {.appendix}

The corpus contains 4,769 documents spanning 59 countries and 6 clause families. Coverage reflects the source mix: SEC EDGAR provides strong US-filed issuance (Latin America, Asia, Europe), PDIP covers African and smaller sovereigns, and NSM adds UK-regulated filings. Gaps exist: NSM has no Latin American documents (UK regulator), and African coverage outside PDIP is thin.

## Extractions by Clause Family

```{python}
#| echo: false
import csv, sys
csv.field_size_limit(sys.maxsize)
import pandas as pd

df = pd.read_csv("data/corpus_summary.csv")
clause_table = (
    df.groupby("clause_family")["count"]
    .sum()
    .reset_index()
    .rename(columns={"clause_family": "Clause Family", "count": "Extractions"})
    .sort_values("Extractions", ascending=False)
)
clause_table["Clause Family"] = clause_table["Clause Family"].str.replace("_", " ").str.title()

from IPython.display import Markdown
Markdown(clause_table.to_markdown(index=False))
```

## Top 20 Countries by Extraction Count

```{python}
#| echo: false
import csv, sys
csv.field_size_limit(sys.maxsize)
import pandas as pd

df = pd.read_csv("data/corpus_summary.csv")
country_table = (
    df.groupby("country")["count"]
    .sum()
    .reset_index()
    .rename(columns={"country": "Country", "count": "Extractions"})
    .sort_values("Extractions", ascending=False)
    .head(20)
)

from IPython.display import Markdown
Markdown(country_table.to_markdown(index=False))
```
